title: Warangal Fort
tagline: Warangal Fort — heritage city of Telangana

overview.short: Warangal Fort is located in Warangal District, Telangana, India.
overview.description: Warangal Fort is located in Warangal District, Telangana, India. It was the capital city of Kakatiya dynasty and the Musunuri Nayakas. The fort appears to have existed since at least the 12th century when it was the capital of the Kakatiyas. The fort has four ornamental gates, known as Kakatiya Kala Thoranam, and also Orugallu fort that originally formed the entrances to a now ruined great Shiva temple.

topPlaces.name: Kakatiya Kala Thoranam
topPlaces.description: Kakatiya Kala Thoranam (also called Warangal Gate) is a historical arch in the Warangal district, of the Indian state of Telangana. The Warangal Fort has four ornamental gates which originally formed the gates to the ruins of the great Shiva temple which are known as Kakatiya Kala Thoranam or Warangal Gates.

topPlaces.name: Shambunipet
topPlaces.description: Shambhunipet is a town in Warangal city in the Indian state of Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Warangal train crash
topPlaces.description: The Warangal train crash occurred on 2 July 2003 in the city of Warangal, Andhra Pradesh, India. The Golconda Express passenger service from Guntur to Secunderabad was scheduled to stop at Warangal station mid-morning, but the driver failed to halt the train.

topPlaces.name: Warangal
topPlaces.description: Warangal (Telugu: [ʋɐrɐŋɡɐl] ), also known as Orugallu, is a city in the Indian state of Telangana and the district headquarters of Warangal district. It is the second largest city in Telangana with a population of 811,844 per 2011 Census of India, and spreading over an 406 km2 (157 sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Late 2011 Telangana protests
topPlaces.description: The late 2011 Telangana protests refers to a chain of protests as part of Telangana movement between September and December 2011. Sakala Janula samme or All Peoples Strike is the biggest protest of all.

topPlaces.name: History of the Telangana movement
topPlaces.description: The history of the Telangana movement refers to the political and social conditions under which the Telangana region was merged with Andhra State to form the state of Andhra Pradesh and the subsequent demands to reverse the merger to form a new state of Telangana from united Andhra Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Pre-2004 Telangana protests
topPlaces.description: The Pre-2004 Telangana protests refers to the movements and agitations related to the Telangana movement that took place before the year 2004. Andhra state and Telangana was merged to form Andhra Pradesh state on 1 November 1956 after providing safeguards to Telangana in the form of Gentlemen's agreement.

topPlaces.name: Early 2011 Telangana protests
topPlaces.description: The early 2011 Telangana protests refers to a chain of events that took place during the early months of 2011, after the Srikrishna committee report was submitted to government of India. These protests are part of Telangana movement.

items.note: Kakatiya Kala Thoranam (also called Warangal Gate) is a historical arch in the Warangal district, of the Indian state of Telangana. The Warangal Fort has four ornamental gates which originally formed the gates to the ruins of the great Shiva temple which are known as Kakatiya Kala Thoranam or Warangal Gates.
Shambhunipet is a town in Warangal city in the Indian state of Telangana.

The Warangal train crash occurred on 2 July 2003 in the city of Warangal, Andhra Pradesh, India. The Golconda Express passenger service from Guntur to Secunderabad was scheduled to stop at Warangal station mid-morning, but the driver failed to halt the train.

Warangal (Telugu: [ʋɐrɐŋɡɐl] ), also known as Orugallu, is a city in the Indian state of Telangana and the district headquarters of Warangal district. It is the second largest city in Telangana with a population of 811,844 per 2011 Census of India, and spreading over an 406 km2 (157 sq mi).

The late 2011 Telangana protests refers to a chain of protests as part of Telangana movement between September and December 2011. Sakala Janula samme or All Peoples Strike is the biggest protest of all.

The history of the Telangana movement refers to the political and social conditions under which the Telangana region was merged with Andhra State to form the state of Andhra Pradesh and the subsequent demands to reverse the merger to form a new state of Telangana from united Andhra Pradesh.

The Pre-2004 Telangana protests refers to the movements and agitations related to the Telangana movement that took place before the year 2004. Andhra state and Telangana was merged to form Andhra Pradesh state on 1 November 1956 after providing safeguards to Telangana in the form of Gentlemen's agreement.

The early 2011 Telangana protests refers to a chain of events that took place during the early months of 2011, after the Srikrishna committee report was submitted to government of India. These protests are part of Telangana movement.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Warangal Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Warangal Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Warangal Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (149 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (131 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Warangal Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Warangal Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Warangal Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Warangal Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Warangal Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kakatiya Kala Thoranam, Shambunipet, Warangal train crash, Warangal, Late 2011 Telangana protests.
